description_en: The first transmitted head, “لَا يُؤمنُونَ,” appears without a displayed numeral.
impact_en: The recovered ordinal layer treats this as head 1 while preserving the witness’s lack of a displayed numeral.

description_en: After displayed 4 “جَدِيد”, the local witness omits the early interval that should run through “خالدون / العقاب / هاد / بمقدار / المتعال / بالنهار / من وال / الثقال / المحال / ضلال / الآصال”, before the sequence resumes near “القهار”.
impact_en: The recovered later-Madani sequence restores this interval explicitly with null source-line fields so that the repair remains auditable.

description_en: The local witness does not transmit the disputed later-Madani head “والنور” at all, and it gives “القهار” without a displayed numeral before resuming with displayed 16 “الْأَمْثَال”.
impact_en: The dataset restores “والنور” from the source entry’s disagreement notice and the checked secondary tabulation, and preserves “القهار” as an unnumbered transmitted head in its recovered position.

description_en: Later in the block, the witness jumps from displayed 22 “الدَّار” to displayed 24 “الدَّار”.
impact_en: The omitted display slot corresponds to the non-baseline disputed head “من كل بَاب”; the later-Madani sequence therefore keeps the three transmitted “الدَّار” heads while preserving the display skip as witness metadata.

description_en: The witness ends with a blank terminal display-total line, rendered locally as “٤ - ٣”.
impact_en: The terminal line is preserved as display metadata only and is not treated as an additional head.

description_en: Because the transmitted block begins unnumbered, loses a long early interval, omits the disputed head “والنور”, transmits “القهار” without a numeral, skips displayed 23, and ends with a blank terminal line, the displayed numerals do not function as a reliable independent ordinal layer.
impact_en: The dataset stores recovered later-Madani ordinals separately from witness display numerals and marks every restoration explicitly.
